title: ADME Pathway Interactive Flow Diagram
description: Animate a drug's journey through Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, and Elimination; pick a route of administration and watch the blood-concentration curve change as molecules flow through the organ stages.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Forensic Toxicology
Pharmacokinetics
ADME
Drug Metabolism
bloomLevel: Understand (L2) — explain the ADME pathway and what happens at each stage.

educational.topic: ADME pharmacokinetic pathway and drug detection windows
learningObjectives: Explain each of the four ADME phases and name the responsible organs.
Describe how route of administration changes the blood-concentration curve.
Relate blood concentration over time to detection windows in blood, urine, and hair.
educational.prerequisites: Basic human anatomy (liver, kidneys); reading a line graph

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Step-through animation: choose a route, play/pause the molecule flow, click a stage to read its description
pedagogical.assessment: Students explain each phase and interpret how the concentration curve differs by route
pedagogical.modelType: Bateman-style absorption/elimination curve over a normalized 24-hour axis
controls: click a stage box to open its description
